page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 architecture instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor. It features 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate a mixed profile. The instance achieves strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and PassMark CPU metrics fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth is moderate, with read-only cached speeds reaching 645.66 GB/sec and uncached large-block writes at 35.27 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, perform poorly, ranking in the bottom 10%, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak.

Given the 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints. It is well-suited for tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving, all of which register average benchmark performance. It also supports moderate Redis operations and small-scale LLM inference. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2 family offers a resource-dense configuration for CPU-bound tasks, minimizing memory overhead costs for workloads that primarily scale with thread count.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-focused instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations align with mid-tier averages. Conversely, single-threaded compression and text processing benchmarks are weak. The platform supports Advanced Vector Extensions and AES cryptography, but lacks advanced matrix extensions or local NVMe storage. This resource configuration is highly cost-efficient for CPU-bound workloads that do not demand extensive memory, making it suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 instance featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM. It offers strong single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-16 features 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: An efficient virtual machine providing sixteen virtual processors and sixteen gigabytes of memory for compute intensive tasks without high memory requirements.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon x86_64 processors
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU acceleration
Average performance for software compilation, ray tracing, and web serving
Cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive, low-memory workloads
